faqs.question: Why are ant problems so common in Cobble Hill brownstones?
faqs.answer: Cobble Hill's historic brownstones feature older foundations, brick mortar gaps, and wood structures that provide easy entry points for ants seeking food and shelter. The neighborhood's mature trees and gardens also support large outdoor ant populations that frequently migrate indoors, especially during spring and summer months.

faqs.question: How quickly can you respond to ant problems in Cobble Hill?
faqs.answer: We offer same-day or next-day service for Cobble Hill residents and businesses. Our Brooklyn-based team can typically arrive within 24 hours of your call to assess the infestation and begin treatment, with most ant control services priced between $350-$550 depending on the severity and size of the affected area.

faqs.question: What types of ants are most common in Cobble Hill?
faqs.answer: Cobble Hill properties commonly experience pavement ants, odorous house ants, and carpenter ants. Carpenter ants are particularly concerning in historic brownstones as they can damage wooden structures. Our technicians identify the specific ant species during inspection to apply the most effective targeted treatment for complete elimination.

faqs.question: Are your ant control treatments safe for families and pets in Cobble Hill homes?
faqs.answer: Yes, we use EPA-approved products and integrate pet-friendly and child-safe treatment methods. Our technicians take extra care in Cobble Hill's family-oriented neighborhood, explaining all safety precautions and offering low-toxicity options. Most residents can remain in their homes during treatment, with specific re-entry times provided based on the products used.
